subject: Computer Networking
title: The Smartphone Revolution
description: A 60-second lesson on The Smartphone Revolution.

hook.text: In 2007, the iPhone put a full web browser in everyone's pocket. Mobile internet usage exploded.

buildup.text: Before smartphones, mobile internet meant tiny WAP pages on flip phones. The experience was painful.

discovery.text: 3G and later 4G networks gave phones enough bandwidth for real websites, video, and app stores.

twist.text: By 2016, mobile traffic surpassed desktop traffic globally. The phone became most people's primary computer.

climax.text: App ecosystems created entirely new industries: ride-sharing, mobile payments, instant messaging at global scale.

punchline.text: The smartphone made the internet portable, personal, and constant.

quiz.question: When did global mobile internet traffic surpass desktop traffic?
